issue.title: Enhancement: Regex toggle in terminal search (E2)

phases.title: Add a regex toggle to TerminalSearch, wire `regex` through every search call, guard invalid patterns
verification: TerminalSearch.tsx exports `isValidRegex(query)` returning true for valid patterns and empty string, false for uncompilable patterns.
A `.*` toggle button renders immediately before the `Aa` button, uses `terminal-search-btn--active` when on, and has `aria-pressed` reflecting `useRegex`.
All three search call sites (debounced effect, Enter/Shift+Enter handler, ▲/▼ buttons) pass `regex: useRegex` in the options object.
When regex mode is on and the pattern is uncompilable, the debounced effect does NOT call the addon, the input shows the `--no-match` red border, and the count span reads "Invalid regex".
The debounced effect's dependency array includes `useRegex`.
`pnpm build` from the repo root passes (tsc -b + vite build) — no unused-symbol or type errors.
`pnpm test` (vitest) passes including the new TerminalSearch test cases.
phases.summary: Added a `.*` regex toggle to TerminalSearch.tsx beside the `Aa` case toggle. New exported `isValidRegex` helper pre-validates patterns; the debounced effect, Enter/Shift+Enter handler, and ▲/▼ buttons all forward `regex: useRegex` to the addon. Uncompilable patterns are caught before reaching the addon — the input gets the existing `--no-match` red border and the count span reads "Invalid regex". New TerminalSearch.test.ts covers formatMatchCount, isNoMatch, and isValidRegex (9 cases). pnpm build clean, pnpm test green.
